# Introduction to EBVs and the EBV Data Portal {#sec-intro}

*Learning objectives: define an EBV; name the six EBV classes; describe why netCDF was chosen; locate a dataset on the portal.*

## What is an Essential Biodiversity Variable?

<!-- TODO: adapt core-concepts.qmd's "What is an EBV?" section from ../core-concepts.qmd -->

## The six EBV classes

<!-- TODO: adapt core-concepts.qmd's callout on canonical classes vs. portal labels -->

## Why netCDF?

<!-- TODO: adapt core-concepts.qmd's "Why netCDF?" section -->

## Touring the EBV Data Portal

We'll use [dataset 30](https://portal.geobon.org/ebv-detail?id=30), *"Global trends in biodiversity (BES-SIM cSAR-iDiv)"* by Inês Martins & Henrique Pereira, as our worked example throughout this tutorial.

<!-- TODO: screenshots of Discover page, dataset 30 detail page, class filters -->

::: callout-note
## A note on datasets

This tutorial uses **two** related datasets by the same research group:

-   **Dataset 30** (above) — our real-world example whenever we talk about the *portal itself*.
-   The small file bundled with the `ebvcube` package — a subset of a *different* dataset (portal ID 1, "Local bird diversity") — which we use for hands-on R code so nothing needs downloading.

Unit 3 shows how to download dataset 30's actual file if you want full end-to-end fidelity.
:::

## Where `ebvcube` fits

<!-- TODO: one paragraph bridging "data on your laptop" -> "data on the portal" -->

**Checkpoint:** Can you open dataset 30's page and identify its EBV class, DOI, and file formats?
